sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Celta Vigo comes into this match with mixed results, securing two wins, two draws, and one loss in their last five games, including an impressive 2-0 victory over Real Madrid. Valencia, on the other hand, has struggled, managing only one win, three draws, and one loss in their recent five, with their defense conceding in most matches. Celta's recent attacking output and home advantage at Estadio Abanca Balaídos give them an edge.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two encounters between these sides, results have been split, with Celta Vigo winning 3-1 at home in August 2024 and Valencia edging a 2-1 victory in February 2025. Both matches saw goals from both teams, indicating a trend of competitive, high-scoring games when they meet.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Celta Vigo, sitting 7th with 23 points, has shown balance with 20 goals scored and 19 conceded, ranking decently in possession and pass accuracy. Valencia, languishing in 17th with 16 points, has struggled offensively with only 16 goals scored while conceding 26, highlighting defensive frailties. Celta’s superior league position and stats suggest they are the favorites.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Celta Vigo, Iago Aspas remains a standout with a 7.52 average Sofascore rating against Valencia historically, contributing goals and assists. Valencia’s Hugo Duro, with 6 goals this season, is a threat upfront despite the team’s struggles, and his overperformance in xG (2.21) could be crucial.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Celta Vigo’s ability to dominate final third entries and convert big chances (41.18% conversion rate) could exploit Valencia’s tendency to concede first in recent games (5/6 matches). Valencia’s reliance on late goals (23% in the 76-90 minute range) might push them to play defensively early, potentially opening spaces for Celta’s attack.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Celta Vigo’s stronger form, home advantage, and Sofascore’s predicted 49% chance of a home win, they are likely to secure the three points. Valencia’s poor away record and defensive issues further tilt the balance in Celta’s favor. Expect a closely contested match with Celta edging it.
